arts.ttl: Norwich City boss makes Abu Kamara plea ahead of Hull City return
arts.sum: Norwich City head coach Johannes Hoff Thorup has pleaded with the club's supporters to be respectful to Abu Kamara as he prepares to make his first return to Carrow Road since joining Hull City on deadline day. London-born Kamara came through the ranks at Norwich and spent 11 years with the Canaries but struggled to force his way into the first team picture, only making his full debut against Stevenage in the Carabao Cup earlier this season - a game in which he scored, and made it clear he wanted to leave by handing in a transfer request.

arts.ttl: Liam Millar lifts the lid on Tim Walter chat as Hull City star eyes success at Norwich City
arts.sum: "We have a very good squad, obviously as a player, I want to play every week, I want to start every week, I think the gaffer knew I was a little bit frustrated with not starting on Saturday (against Cardiff). Liam Millar admits he was frustrated not to have started Saturday's win over Cardiff City, but responded in the perfect way by capping a fine display with his first Hull City goal on Tuesday night in the 3-1 win at QPR .

arts.ttl: Marti Cifuentes overlooked an important detail in his Hull City transfer jibe
arts.sum: While City have spent money this summer, on the likes of Liam Millar, Charlie Hughes, Abu Kamara and Mohamed Belloumi, one thing many opposing managers conveniently won't mention is that the club brought in substantial transfer fees to help fund their incomings. Perhaps it's unsurprising that City's recruitment - which saw 17 new players arrive after more than 20 left at the end of last season - finally got raised by an opposite manager, especially after a defeat which saw Walter able to bring the likes of Oscar Zambrano, Marvin Mehlem and Abdus Omur off the bench, showing the strength in depth he now has at his disposal.

arts.ttl: Zambrano back in: Hull City team news and predicted XI to face Norwich City
arts.sum: Hull City head to Carrow Road this weekend to face Norwich City in their latest Championship outing. Hull… The post Zambrano back in: Hull City team news and predicted XI to face Norwich City appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Daunting Norwich City form provides Hull City with chance to make their mark
arts.sum: The Carrow Road clash kicks off a run of three crucial games for Walter's men, albeit punctured by the international break, with home games against the top two - Sunderland and Burnley - to come straight after, and Goodman believes it'll be easier to judge City once those games have come and gone. After a winless start to the season, City have beaten Stoke City, Cardiff City and QPR with the latter coming at Loftus Road on Tuesday night, with Cody Drameh, Chris Bedia and Liam Millar all netting in a 3-1 success .
Live updates from Norwich City head coach Johannes Hoff Thorup's pre-Hull City press conference.

arts.ttl: Predicted Hull City team to face Norwich City as Tim Walter faces selection conundrum
arts.sum: The German head coach made three changes to his side at QPR on Tuesday night, bringing in Xavier Simons, Liam Millar and Kasey Palmer at the expense of Oscar Zambrano, Marvin Mehlem and Abdus Omur, with the trio dropping to the bench, before coming on in the second half of City's 3-1 Loftus Road success . And they worked, with Simons delivering a steady performance in the middle of the park, and Millar displaying his best performance yet in Black and Amber and bagging his first goal, but the clash with the Canaries will pose an altogether more difficult challenge, though Zambrano's introduction with Mehlem in the second half saw City wrestle back control having lost it.

arts.ttl: ‘Can do more’ – Hull City new boy tipped to get even better by Tim Walter ahead of Norwich City trip
arts.sum: Hull City boss Tim Walter believes the best is yet to come from striker Chris Bedia. Hull City… The post ‘Can do more’ – Hull City new boy tipped to get even better by Tim Walter ahead of Norwich City trip appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: 'Carnage' - The Hull City assessment that nails Tim Walter's heart-attack football - Hull Live
arts.sum: EFL pundit Adrian Clarke has praised Hull City and manager Tim Walter after watching the Tigers earn a 3-1 win at QPR on Tuesday night to notch up three straight victories in the Championship. City scored twice in the first half through Cody Drameh - his first in Black and Amber - and Chris Bedia before Rangers pulled one back from the penalty spot after Lewie Coyle was penalised for handball, but the game was put to bed 20 minutes from time when the excellent Liam Millar rattled in his first goal for the club.

arts.ttl: Significant Premier League transfer rule change that could impact Hull City
arts.sum: Previously, the EFL has worked with the Premier League to ensure symmetry across the leagues with this summer's window closing on August 30, at 11pm, but an August 15 start date would still come a week into the EFL season, which is expected to begin the weekend before around August 9 and 10. Managers across all leagues have voiced frustration previously about the window closing after the start of the season, including City boss Tim Walter, who felt it should be shut before the season kicked off.
